message:
feat(palette): evidence ledger panel with Bayesian score inspection (1mfw3.1.5)

Add an evidence ledger overlay panel for the command palette that shows
why each result ranked where it did.  The panel displays match type,
Bayesian score, individual evidence entries (kind, Bayes factor,
direction), combined BF, posterior probability, and scorer cache stats.

Changes:
- Enable evidence tracking on CommandPalette at initialization
- Add show_palette_evidence state field with reset on all close paths
  (PaletteClosed, PaletteActionExecuted, Dismiss, Esc)
- Add PaletteEvidenceToggled message variant
- Intercept Alt+E in palette interceptor to toggle evidence panel
- Add render_palette_evidence() with centered lower panel, evidence
  entries from selected_match(), and scorer_stats() footer
- Empty-state messaging when no match is selected
- 6 new tests: default off, toggle, reset on close/execute/esc, tracking

Also includes cockpit state contracts and InspectorTab expansion from
concurrent work on 1mfw3.3.1/1mfw3.3.2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","title":"Migrate cass command palette to FrankenTUI evidence-driven palette","description":"Workstream .1: Migrate cass command palette to FrankenTUI evidence-driven palette.

Background:
cass currently uses a local PaletteState + custom overlay renderer. This delivers basic filtering/navigation but lacks explainability, rich match-mode controls, and built-in search quality telemetry. FrankenTUI already ships command-palette primitives with Bayesian evidence and match-type filters.

Goals:
1) Replace custom overlay behavior with ftui command_palette capabilities where practical.
2) Preserve cass action semantics and shortcut discoverability.
3) Expose match evidence and query-latency signals so users can trust ranking output.
4) Maintain deterministic keyboard-first operation and low-latency feel.

In scope:
- Action model mapping from current PaletteAction catalog.
- Rendering + interaction migration in app overlay path.
- Match filter controls and evidence ledger UI.
- Regression tests for palette behavior.

Out of scope:
- Unrelated command renaming churn.
- New non-cass commands not tied to current operator workflows.

Validation expectations:
- Existing palette tests continue to pass or are replaced with stronger equivalents.
- Manual checks confirm no input lag and no focus-trap regressions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.263824178Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34.283183425Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.263824178Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation guidance: keep the migration adapter-centric. Avoid scattering palette-specific branching throughout unrelated app update/render paths. Preserve all existing action semantics first, then add evidence/filter enhancements in incremental steps to minimize regression surface.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.1","title":"Palette migration discovery and action mapping contract","description":"Subtask .1.1

Objective:
Create an explicit mapping between current cass palette semantics and FrankenTUI command_palette concepts so migration is deliberate rather than ad hoc.

Deliverables:
- Action inventory with grouping (theme/filter/saved-view/analytics/screenshot/macro/sources).
- Mapping table to target action identifiers and display metadata.
- Interaction contract for keyboard navigation, focus trap behavior, and close/execute semantics.

Rationale:
A clear mapping contract de-risks migration by preventing accidental command loss, hint regressions, or semantic drift.

Done when:
All current palette actions are mapped and no action is left without an explicit target behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:43.298717896Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:51:17.807563772Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T00:51:17.807541641Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:43.298717896Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.2","title":"Implement palette adapter layer for cass action dispatch","description":"Subtask .1.2

Objective:
Introduce a focused adapter translating FrankenTUI palette selection events into existing cass message/action dispatch, preserving business semantics.

Deliverables:
- Adapter structs/functions that bridge palette result -> CassMsg or equivalent action path.
- Compatibility handling for slot-based actions and existing shortcuts.
- Clear extension points for future palette actions.

Rationale:
A dedicated adapter isolates migration complexity and prevents palette widget internals from leaking into broad app logic.

Done when:
Palette action execution parity is maintained with minimal changes to downstream command handlers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:57.119725562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:07:51.705996907Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T01:07:51.705974676Z","close_reason":"Adapter layer complete: PaletteResult enum, dispatch(), execute_selected(), palette_result_to_cmd() + 28 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:57.119725562Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.413671666Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","title":"Replace custom palette overlay rendering with ftui command_palette widget","description":"Subtask .1.3

Objective:
Swap manual overlay rendering path to use FrankenTUI command_palette rendering primitives while preserving cass visual language.

Deliverables:
- Overlay rendering integration in app view path.
- Selection/highlight/scroll behavior delegated to widget logic.
- Styling alignment with cass theme system.

Rationale:
Using battle-tested widget behavior reduces bespoke rendering bugs and unlocks advanced palette capabilities.

Done when:
The palette opens/renders/navigates through widget-backed code rather than manual row formatting.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:06.050805507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:11:55.610501357Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:06.050805507Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.695325368Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","title":"Add match-type filters and explicit filter controls in palette","description":"Subtask .1.4

Objective:
Expose match-mode filtering controls (all/exact/prefix/word-start/substring/fuzzy) in cass palette UX.

Deliverables:
- Keyboard controls for filter mode cycle and direct mode selection.
- Visual indicator of active filter mode.
- Graceful behavior when filters produce zero results.

Rationale:
Filter controls make ranking behavior transparent and increase precision for expert users.

Done when:
Users can intentionally select match modes and observe deterministic result set changes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.358901842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.981454650Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.358901842Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.981368790Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Rationale:
Explainability builds operator trust and reduces perceived randomness in fuzzy search.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","title":"Palette migration regression tests and deterministic behavior lock-in","description":"Objective:
Build comprehensive automated test coverage for palette migration with both unit-level guarantees and end-to-end behavior validation.

Deliverables:
- Extensive unit tests for open/close, navigation, filtering, execution dispatch, and deterministic ordering behavior.
- Dedicated e2e script(s) exercising palette lifecycle flows (open -> type -> filter mode -> navigate -> execute -> close).
- Structured detailed logging from e2e runs (JSONL or equivalent) including query, match_count, selected_action, latency, and assertion outcomes.
- Human-readable test summary artifact linking failed assertions to exact scenario steps.

Rationale:
Palette is a high-frequency operator surface; reliability and debuggability must be proven at both logic and interaction layers.

Done when:
Unit tests and e2e scripts both pass consistently, with rich logs sufficient for fast post-failure diagnosis.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.959385335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:06:51.492830905Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.959385335Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:57.406086560Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:58.383079912Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:58.693489133Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","title":"Add palette query latency instrumentation and micro-bench toggle","description":"Objective:
Add low-overhead latency instrumentation for palette query updates and an opt-in micro-bench mode to detect performance regressions.

Deliverables:
- Last-query latency capture.
- Optional benchmark mode with throughput signal (queries/sec).
- Visual budget indicator for query responsiveness.

Rationale:
Making latency visible helps prevent UX regressions as command catalog/features grow.

Done when:
Operators can quickly verify palette responsiveness from within the TUI.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:23.007851279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:55.597488555Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:23.007851279Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:55.597408966Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","title":"Enable BOCPD resize coalescer and surface resize decisions in UI","description":"Workstream .2: Enable BOCPD resize coalescer and surface resize decisions in UI.

Background:
cass currently runs on ftui runtime with budget + mouse enabled, but does not fully wire advanced ProgramConfig knobs for resize evidence and regime-aware coalescing. FrankenTUI includes a resize coalescer with BOCPD support and decision logs.

Goals:
1) Wire ProgramConfig with explicit resize coalescer settings (logging + BOCPD).
2) Capture/summarize resize decisions in cass-facing model state.
3) Surface resize behavior in inspector so users can diagnose resize storms.
4) Keep behavior deterministic and robust across fullscreen/inline/macro paths.

In scope:
- run_tui_ftui config unification and upgrade.
- Evidence plumbing and summary model.
- Inspector presentation of resize regime/action/evidence.
- Test coverage for resize storms and fallback behavior.

Out of scope:
- Full explainability cockpit UX (tracked in .3).

Validation expectations:
- No regressions in macro record/playback paths.
- Resize behavior remains stable under rapid terminal changes.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.384452802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34.870097395Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.384452802Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation guidance: treat runtime config parity as non-negotiable. Any knob added for standard mode must be reviewed for record/playback + inline/fullscreen paths. Evidence wiring should fail soft (graceful empty state) rather than fail closed (broken UI path).","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34Z"}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","title":"Enable BOCPD resize coalescer and surface resize decisions in UI","description":"Workstream .2: Enable BOCPD resize coalescer and surface resize decisions in UI.

Background:
cass currently runs on ftui runtime with budget + mouse enabled, but does not fully wire advanced ProgramConfig knobs for resize evidence and regime-aware coalescing. FrankenTUI includes a resize coalescer with BOCPD support and decision logs.

Goals:
1) Wire ProgramConfig with explicit resize coalescer settings (logging + BOCPD).
2) Capture/summarize resize decisions in cass-facing model state.
3) Surface resize behavior in inspector so users can diagnose resize storms.
4) Keep behavior deterministic and robust across fullscreen/inline/macro paths.

In scope:
- run_tui_ftui config unification and upgrade.
- Evidence plumbing and summary model.
- Inspector presentation of resize regime/action/evidence.
- Test coverage for resize storms and fallback behavior.

Out of scope:
- Full explainability cockpit UX (tracked in .3).

Validation expectations:
- No regressions in macro record/playback paths.
- Resize behavior remains stable under rapid terminal changes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.384452802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T02:04:13.626458707Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T02:04:13.626433840Z","close_reason":"all children closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.384452802Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation guidance: treat runtime config parity as non-negotiable. Any knob added for standard mode must be reviewed for record/playback + inline/fullscreen paths. Evidence wiring should fail soft (graceful empty state) rather than fail closed (broken UI path).","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","title":"Unify ProgramConfig construction across standard, record, and playback paths","description":"Objective:
Refactor run_tui_ftui so all execution modes share one explicit ProgramConfig construction path.

Deliverables:
- Common config builder with mode-specific deltas only where required.
- Consistent runtime knobs across standard, macro recording, and playback paths.
- Reduced configuration drift risk.

Why this matters:
Without a unified builder, advanced runtime options are easy to wire in one path and forget in others, creating invisible behavioral inconsistency.

Done when:
All launch paths consume the same config baseline and differences are intentional and documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:34.183111790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:02:45.478633008Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T01:02:45.478609584Z","close_reason":"Unified: all 3 paths (standard/record/playback) share one ProgramConfig + Program::with_config entry point","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:34.183111790Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","title":"Enable BOCPD + logging CoalescerConfig profile for cass runtime","description":"Objective:
Select and wire an explicit resize coalescer profile with BOCPD enabled and decision logging turned on.

Deliverables:
- CoalescerConfig settings for steady/burst/deadline behavior.
- BOCPD enabled with sane defaults for cass interaction profile.
- Logging configuration suitable for UI-facing evidence summaries.

Why this matters:
Regime-aware resize behavior is core to responsiveness under resize storms; logging is required for explainability and debugging.

Done when:
Runtime is using explicit BOCPD/logging settings in all relevant launch modes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:47.528311592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:27:07.611616912Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T01:27:07.611590924Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:47.528311592Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.009687150Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.3","title":"Wire resize decision evidence capture path into cass model layer","description":"Objective:
Capture resize decision/evidence signals emitted by runtime and make them available to cass model state.

Deliverables:
- Evidence ingestion hook(s) from runtime output.
- Parsing/normalization into typed cass-side structures.
- Error-tolerant handling of missing/partial evidence events.

Why this matters:
Evidence must exist in model memory before any inspector/cockpit panel can explain adaptive behavior.

Done when:
cass model can access latest resize decision and associated evidence summary during rendering.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:55.716818104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:35:44.142529952Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T01:35:44.142502932Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:55.716818104Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.331718115Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.4","title":"Define cass resize evidence summary state and rendering-ready schema","description":"Objective:
Design minimal but sufficient cass-side state for resize evidence presentation.

Deliverables:
- Structs for latest action/regime/timing/evidence contributions.
- Ring buffer or snapshot policy for recent resize decisions.
- Backfill/default semantics when evidence is absent.

Why this matters:
A stable local schema decouples UI rendering from raw runtime event shape and supports safe evolution.

Done when:

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.6","title":"Add deterministic resize storm tests and evidence integrity checks","description":"Objective:
Validate resize behavior and evidence integrity with comprehensive unit and e2e coverage under deterministic and storm-like conditions.

Deliverables:
- Unit tests for repeated, oscillating, and burst resize sequences with deterministic expected transitions.
- E2e resize-storm scripts that drive realistic terminal resize patterns and verify applied actions/regimes.
- Detailed structured logging artifacts from e2e runs (decision stream, evidence contributions, regime transitions, timings).
- Failure-focused summary output that pinpoints first divergence from expected decision behavior.

Rationale:
Resize logic is a core responsiveness primitive and must remain stable, explainable, and reproducible.

Done when:
Both unit and e2e resize suites pass with sufficient telemetry to diagnose any future regressions quickly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.863195535Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:06:51.942417584Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.863195535Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:01.046780574Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:00.650617356Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","title":"Verify macro and inline/fullscreen parity after resize config upgrade","description":"Objective:
Prove runtime parity across macro record/playback and inline/fullscreen modes using scenario-driven e2e validation.

Deliverables:
- Mode matrix e2e scripts (standard, record, playback, inline, fullscreen).
- Detailed logging per scenario (mode, config knobs, observed resize behavior, replay/record lifecycle events, key assertions).
- Regression report capturing intentional vs unintentional behavioral differences.
- Fixes or guardrails for any parity drift.

Rationale:
Configuration divergence across runtime modes is a common source of hidden production bugs.

Done when:
Parity checks pass across supported modes with explicit logs proving equivalent behavior where expected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.961042056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:48:51.685416831Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T01:48:51.685385362Z","close_reason":"Parity proven via build_resize_config() extraction + 4 matrix tests. All launch modes (standard, inline, record, playback) share identical BOCPD/coalescer/evidence config.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.961042056Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:01.504699105Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:01.845945212Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","title":"Upgrade inspector to explainability cockpit (diff/resize/budget timeline)","description":"Workstream .3: Upgrade inspector to explainability cockpit (diff/resize/budget timeline).

Background:
Current inspector has useful timing/layout/hit-region tabs but limited causal explanation. FrankenTUI showcase demonstrates explainability cockpit patterns that unify diff strategy, resize regime, and budget/degradation decisions.

Goals:
1) Expand inspector into a practical explainability cockpit.
2) Provide compact timeline context for adaptive runtime decisions.
3) Make performance/debug investigations possible without external log spelunking.

In scope:
- New/expanded inspector tab architecture.
- Panels for diff strategy summary, resize summary, budget/degradation summary.
- Timeline feed correlating major decision events.
- Responsive overlay behavior for constrained terminals.

Out of scope:
- Offline analytics dashboards outside TUI runtime loop.

Validation expectations:
- Cockpit renders safely across degradation levels and terminal sizes.
- Added data never crashes when evidence is absent/partial.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.853555598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:35.627601254Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.853555598Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:02.157691206Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":205,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Design note: cockpit UX should optimize for diagnosis latency. Prefer compact, high-signal fields and causal timeline snippets over verbose text dumps. The best cockpit answers: what changed, why it changed, and what the system is doing now.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:35Z"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.1","title":"Define explainability cockpit information architecture and data contract","description":"Objective:
Specify cockpit panel structure, data requirements, and fallback behavior before implementation.

Deliverables:
- Panel taxonomy (diff/resize/budget/timeline).
- Required fields and source of truth per panel.
- Empty/error-state policies for missing telemetry.

Why this matters:
A clear IA contract prevents fragmented UI and unbounded scope during implementation.

Done when:
Cockpit implementation can proceed against a stable, explicit panel/data blueprint.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:22.196005382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:22.196005382Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:22.196005382Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.3","title":"Finalize cockpit overlay/full modes and responsive sizing behavior","description":"Objective:
Finalize how cockpit behaves as compact overlay and as fuller inspector surface across terminal sizes.

Deliverables:
- Sizing constraints and truncation policy.
- Layout behavior for narrow/medium/wide terminals.
- Readability safeguards for low-height windows.

Why this matters:
Explainability tooling must remain usable in realistic constrained terminals.

Done when:
Cockpit remains legible and non-disruptive across supported terminal dimensions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:40.289091815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:04.319192425Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:40.289091815Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:04.069247799Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:04.319111204Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:03.807415454Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.4","title":"Cockpit regression tests and no-panic guarantees across degradation levels","description":"Objective:
Add strong tests guaranteeing cockpit reliability and graceful behavior across terminal sizes and degradation modes.

Deliverables:
- Comprehensive unit tests for panel builders, fallback policies, and no-panic rendering across representative dimensions.
- E2e cockpit scripts covering tab switching, overlay/full modes, and missing-evidence scenarios.
- Structured run logging from e2e tests (active tab, visible panels, fallback decisions, evidence availability, timing snapshots).
- Failure-focused summary that identifies first divergence and likely root cause.

Why this matters:
Debug tooling must never destabilize the primary application loop, and failures must be diagnosable quickly.

Done when:
Cockpit unit and e2e suites pass consistently with rich logs that make regressions straightforward to diagnose.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:40.374157119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:21.072295452Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:40.374157119Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:04.559800956Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:05.052326218Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:05.293477130Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:04.807460835Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.5","title":"Build budget/degradation explainability panel","description":"Objective:
Render frame-budget and degradation-level signals in a compact but interpretable cockpit panel.

Deliverables:
- Budget target/usage and degradation level summary.
- Trend indicators where available (e.g., recent pressure).
- Clear warning styling for exceeded budgets.

Why this matters:
Users need immediate clarity on why visual fidelity changes under load.

Done when:
Cockpit makes adaptive degradation behavior understandable at a glance.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:40.601968987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:03.008611506Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:40.601968987Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:03.008555030Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
